lecture_character: partial / fragment — appears to be the closing minutes of a class session before a break, covering Stout and Doty reference, Jominy hardenability test, heat treatments of steel (surface hardening, carburizing, nitriding, boriding), martensite transformation, shape memory alloys, ending with break announcement before presentations
key_references_mentioned: Stout and Doty, Weldability of Steels
Charlie Brooks's book (Principles of the Heat Treatment of Plain Carbon and Low Alloy Steels)
MIT Spectrum (alumni magazine, anonymous colleague reference)

tom_spoken_slips_bracketed.note: Likely captioner garble of 'dentists' but bracketed conservatively

section_boundaries_proposed_for_layer3.s3: Aside on the colleague who said we don't know much about steel (p3)

section_boundaries_proposed_for_layer3.s4: Surface hardening and heat treatments of steel (p4-p6)

section_boundaries_proposed_for_layer3.s5: Martensite transformation and shape memory alloys (p7-p8)

notes: Short fragment (~6 minutes). Tom is finishing a teaching unit and signaling a transition to student presentations after a break. Hydrogen embrittlement, signaled at end, is deferred to next session.
